title: Quantum Gravimetry Pilot for Saudi Geological Survey
entry_mechanism: Saudi Geological Survey partnership with quantum sensing company for pilot deployment
executive_summary:
Quantum gravimetry — using quantum atomic interferometry to measure gravitational field variations with extraordinary precision — has immediate practical applications in geological survey, oil and gas exploration, and underground infrastructure mapping. Quantum gravimeters can detect density variations in the subsurface with sensitivity 100x greater than classical gravimeters, enabling more accurate identification of hydrocarbon reservoirs, mineral deposits, and underground voids.

Saudi Arabia's geological survey requirements — mapping the Arabian Shield mineral potential, characterising Aramco's mature oilfield subsurface, and detecting underground water aquifers — create an immediate high-value application for quantum gravimetry. The technology is commercially available from a small number of companies (Muquans in France, M-Squared Lasers in UK, AOSense in USA).

Node QT-2 targets Saudi Arabia's first quantum sensing deployment: a quantum gravimetry pilot with the Saudi Geological Survey (SGS) to characterise the Arabian Shield's mineral potential using quantum-precision measurements, with transfer of operational capability to Saudi geophysicists.

companies.name: Muquans (iXblue)
companies.rationale: Muquans produces the most commercially mature quantum gravimeters and has government deployment experience. French technology, non-ITAR.

companies.name: M-Squared Lasers
companies.rationale: M-Squared builds quantum sensing platforms for government applications. UK technology with active government partnerships.

companies.name: AOSense
companies.rationale: AOSense produces atomic interferometry sensors for gravimetry applications.

companies.name: Safran Sensing Technologies
companies.rationale: Safran is developing quantum inertial sensors with military and survey applications.

companies.name: Q.ANT (Trumpf Group)
companies.rationale: Q.ANT develops compact quantum sensors for industrial and survey applications.

partners.name: Saudi Geological Survey (SGS)
partners.current: Classical gravimetry for mineral exploration; Aramco geophysical data sharing
partners.upgrade: SGS as pilot operator; Muquans quantum gravimeter deployment at 3 Arabian Shield survey sites; Saudi geophysicist training; pilot data comparison with classical survey

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Geosciences
partners.current: Subsurface characterisation; gravity surveys for EOR planning
partners.upgrade: Quantum gravimetry for Aramco mature field subsurface mapping; EOR planning improvement from quantum-precision gravity data

partners.name: KACST / Quantum Physics Research
partners.current: Some quantum sensing research; atomic physics research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST quantum gravimetry research programme; Saudi quantum sensing laboratory; Arabian Shield geological quantum survey programme

partners.name: KFUPM Geoscience / Physics

partners.upgrade: Quantum sensing specialisation for geophysics applications; SGS-KFUPM quantum gravimetry programme; Saudi quantum geophysicist pipeline
